title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Root Cause & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the complete lifecycle of FIX-467, a bug where creating the first library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. The root cause was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully resolve the issue. The tour follows the investigation in data-flow order: from the initial user report, through successive agent triage runs, to the final corrected token-selection logic and verification screenshots.

steps.description: This is the entry point: the original ticket FIX-467 filed by the user. The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. Screenshots were attached as evidence of the failure. A critical constraint is noted here — the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This ticket context file is the seed of the entire investigation and sets the scope: the problem is environment-specific (staging) and involves the artifact creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section (line 26) records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run focused on understanding the failure mode from the attached screenshots and the available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping the problem before making any changes.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Triage

steps.description: By this third run, the agent had made a first attempt at a fix and reported changes ready, including verification screenshots. However, the user (Usher) replied that the issue persisted (line 44 onward). This is the critical pivot point in the investigation: the user clarifies that for NetSuite file cabinet repos, the system must use the built-in Helix system token — not the user's own authentication token — when creating repositories. The earlier fix had addressed a surface symptom but missed this root cause in the token-selection logic. The agent's reply confirms it now understands the distinction between user tokens and the Helix system token.
steps.title: Step 3: First Fix Attempt & Root Cause Identified

steps.description: This run contains the full accumulated discussion history and represents the agent's second major attempt at a fix after the root cause was correctly identified. At this point the agent understands that repository creation for NetSuite file cabinet repos must route through the Helix system token path, not the user's token. This ticket.md serves as the source of truth for the corrected understanding and the fix rationale that drove subsequent code changes. Data flow: corrected token-selection logic → repository creation → artifact pipeline → successful library ticket creation.
steps.title: Step 4: Corrected Understanding & Second Fix Attempt

steps.description: This is the CodeTour walkthrough generated during the second major fix run. It encodes the agent's understanding of the bug history and code change rationale into a structured, navigable format. The tour's description field confirms the root cause: the system was using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories for NetSuite file cabinet repos. This artifact demonstrates that the agent had internalized the correct diagnosis before generating code fixes.
steps.title: Step 5: Intraview Walkthrough (Run cmp7kjf3)

steps.description: A refined version of the CodeTour walkthrough generated in a subsequent run. The description here is more precise: it explicitly states the root cause as token-selection logic and notes that multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully fix the issue. The steps in this tour follow data-flow order, mirroring the actual investigation path. This file confirms the agent's analysis was stabilizing across runs and converging on a complete, accurate fix.
steps.title: Step 6: Refined Intraview Walkthrough (Run cmp7n46rs)

steps.description: This is the most complete ticket context file, representing the final run in the series. It contains the full discussion history including all agent responses, user feedback, and the confirmed root cause. By this run the fix has been correctly scoped: the Helix system token must be used — not the user's token — when creating repositories for NetSuite file cabinet repos. This file is the definitive record of the bug's lifecycle and should be the primary reference for any future regression investigation.
steps.title: Step 7: Final Run Ticket Context (Full History)

steps.description: This is the final and most complete CodeTour walkthrough artifact produced by the agent. Its description field provides the authoritative one-paragraph summary of the entire FIX-467 lifecycle: the bug (no artifacts on first library ticket creation in staging), the root cause (user token used instead of Helix system token for NetSuite file cabinet repos), the resolution path (multiple agent runs to surface and fix the token-selection logic), and the verification approach (screenshots attached to the ticket). All major code paths in the artifact creation pipeline now correctly route NetSuite file cabinet repository creation through the Helix system token, ensuring the library is created regardless of the user's own token state.
steps.title: Step 8: Final Intraview Walkthrough & Summary
